{{Quote|In the name of the Emperor, I claim this planet as Imperial territory.|An Imperial officer|<ref name="Tales19" />}}
'''Gibbela''' was a remote [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]]. It was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifting]] [[Dazouri]] [[Species/Legends|species]], members of which engaged in simple [[Farmer/Legends|farming]] on Gibbela.

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], an Imperial detachment was [[Mission to Gibbela|sent]] to Gibbela to [[Scout/Legends|scout]] the planet before its planned [[Colonist/Legends|colonization]]. Landing at a [[Unidentified farm (Gibbela)|farm]] of [[Unidentified Dazouri farmer|one]] of the local Dazouri, the [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Gibbela)|officer]] leading the Imperial force claimed the planet for the Empire and demanded the farmer's surrender. In response, however, the Dazouri transformed into a large, ferocious [[Death/Legends|killer]] and wiped out the Imperials, subsequently repurposing their leftover equipment as farming tools.

==Description==
Gibbela was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Tales19" /> located in the [[Gibbela system/Legends|Gibbela system]], a part of the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA" /> An isolated world, Gibbela was located far from even minor [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]].<ref name="Tales19" />

==History==
{{Quote|Surrender and no harm will come to you.|An Imperial officer, to a Dazouri farmer|<ref name="Tales19" />}}
At some point during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], that government made plans to [[Colonist/Legends|colonize]] Gibbela. A small Imperial [[Scout/Legends|scout]] detachment,<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|340|Gibbela}}</ref> consisting of a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] carrying an [[Unidentified Imperial officer (Gibbela)|Imperial officer]] and a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] escort,<ref name="Tales19" /> was [[Mission to Gibbela|dispatched]] to the planet, expecting little resistance. The initial fly-by showed the Imperial force several non-threatening [[Farmer/Legends|farmers]] of<ref name="CSWE" /> the native [[Dazouri]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="Shapeshifters" />

[[File:Gibbela_transforms.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Imperial detachment on Gibbela about to be destroyed by the transformed Dazouri farmer]]
The Imperial [[Landing craft/Legends|starship]] landed at the [[Unidentified farm (Gibbela)|farm]] of [[Unidentified Dazouri farmer|one]] of the locals. Emerging from the [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], the [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] addressed the farmer, claiming Gibbela as [[Imperial Space|Imperial territory]] in the name of the [[Palpatine/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and demanding the Dazouri's surrender. To the detachment's surprise, the farmer then underwent the transformation<ref name="Tales19" /> characteristic to the Dazouri,<ref name="Shapeshifters" /> turning into a ferocious [[Death/Legends|killer]] that wiped out the outmatched Imperial force. Subsequently, the Dazouri repurposed the Imperials' [[DLT-19 heavy blaster rifle/Legends|weapons]], [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|armor]], and even their landing craft as farming equipment.<ref name="Tales19" /> Between [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], Gibbela fell within the territory controlled by the [[Pentastar Alignment]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Inhabitants==
A primitive agricultural planet,<ref name="Tales19" /> Gibbela was the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the Dazouri,<ref name="Shapeshifters" /> a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Changeling/Legends|shapeshifters]]. While the Dazouri normally appeared as short and helpless [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoids]], when provoked they would transform into three-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-tall homicidal and frenzied<ref name="Planets">''[[The Star Wars Planets Collection]]''</ref> [[Diet/Legends|predators]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Due to the ferocious nature of the Dazouri, [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] tended to avoid visiting Gibbela.<ref name="Tales19" /> During the reign of the Galactic Empire, Gibbela was inhabited by several Dazouri farmers,<ref name="CSWE" /> one of whom destroyed an Imperial scouting party.<ref name="Tales19" />

==Locations==
One Dazouri maintained a [[Farm/Legends|farm]] on Gibbela, which consisted of a large tent and numerous circles of cultivated [[Plant/Legends|plants]]. Each circle consisted of a tripod-supported [[Unidentified Gibbela fruit tree|fruit tree]] that was surrounded by a ring of [[Unidentified Gibbela crop plant|crop plants]]. The farm also utilized at least one [[Scarecrow/Legends|scarecrow]]. After the farmer repelled an Imperial scouting party that visited Gibbela, the Imperial's starship was repurposed into another building at the farm.<ref name="Tales19" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gibbela was introduced in "[[The Value of Proper Intelligence to Any Successful Military Campaign is Not to Be Underestimated]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story written by [[Ken Lizzl]], illustrated by [[Lucas Marangon]], and published in the [[Star Wars Tales 19|nineteenth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' comic-book series<ref name="Tales19" /> on [[May 14]], [[2004]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53997/star_wars_tales_1999_19|text=Star Wars Tales (1999) #19}}</ref> With respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, [[Lucasfilm]] considered plausible stories in [[Star Wars Tales 1|''Star Wars Tales'' 1]]–[[Star Wars Tales 20|20]] to be [[Canon|S-canon]],<ref name="Tales1-20">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM|quote=Consider everything that's not completely outrageous or intentionally comic as S-canon. If it's referenced in another non-''Tales'' source, then elevate it to C-canon.}}</ref> or "secondary" canon,<ref name="Canon">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?forumID=24&start=15&threadID=152583&msRange=15#5706120|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2004-01-21|time=9:16 AM}}</ref> by default until they were referenced in a non-''Tales'' source, at which point the subject matter was elevated to C-canon,<ref name="Tales1-20" /> or "continuity" canon.<ref name="Canon" />

The events of the comic story from ''Star Wars Tales'' 19 were later mentioned in an entry for Gibbela in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Gibbela [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square L-5.<ref name="TEA" />

==Appearances==
